note: The official syllabus applies to students admitted in 2025-26 and thereafter. This supplement records the 210 unique coded courses explicitly published for the English Language and Linguistics Major, the Professional Core in English Language Education, and the applicable language enhancement routes; it is not the complete universe of courses a student may use toward graduation. The open 60-credit free-elective requirement, uncoded Common Core and artificial-intelligence-literacy requirements, non-credit national education and national security requirements, and other unspecified non-credit courses are excluded. CEDU9004 is excluded because the syllabus assigns it only to candidates majoring in Chinese Language, Literature and Culture. BBED6721-BBED6730, BBED6732 and BBED6782 are excluded because they are listed only as optional free electives, not as prescribed English-path courses. The 16 Community-based Professional Practicum courses are the options currently published in the syllabus, but the Faculty states that they may not be offered every year, that the pool may change, and that other opportunities may be announced. Recommended Year and Semester values appear only where the official five-year structure or course section states them; no timing is inferred from course codes.
